items.title: Papers: Arsenal preparing offer for Aston Villa defender Konsa
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Saturday's newspapers...

items.title: Arsenal want Villa's Rogers - Saturday's gossip
items.summary: Arsenal want Morgan Rogers, Mohamed Salah is set to join Besiktas, Manchester United are interested in Neco Williams, plus more.

items.title: Does Bryson improve his lie? DeChambeau given two-shot penalty after rules drama!
items.summary: Bryson DeChambeau was given a two-shot penalty after inadvertently improving his lie in thick rough on the fifth hole at The Open.

items.title: Michael Owen: Beating Mexico was the worst thing for England.. it gave Thomas Tuchel the blueprint for World Cup failure
items.summary: MICHAEL OWEN has claimed England beating Mexico set up their eventual downfall against Argentina. The Three Lions showed off a superb back-to-the-wall rear-guard action against Mexico at the Azteca Stadium in the last 16 after going down to ten men. But Owen believes that bringing the same ideas int

items.title: Forget Messi, Rodri will inspire ‘boring’ Spain to beat Argentina in World Cup final… my mate just wins matches
items.summary: I’M PUTTING my money on Spain to win this World Cup. It’s because of how much ball possession they have and the way they control the game. They do that better than anyone else. Of course, I can’t totally write off Argentina — it would be very silly of me to do that to a team...

items.title: Saints boss Eckert reveals police protection amid threats during 'Spygate'
items.summary: Southampton boss Tonda Eckert has revealed he received police protection amid threats during the Spygate scandal.

items.title: England’s World Cup foe Enzo Fernandez hints he’s UNHAPPY at Chelsea with Real Madrid transfer on the cards
items.summary: ENZO FERNANDEZ dropped hints about his unhappiness at Chelsea — even as he claimed life now feels “perfect”. The Blues midfielder helped end England’s World Cup hopes with the late equaliser in Argentina’s 2-1 semi-final win on Wednesday. His joy at making tomorrow’s final against Spain came after a

items.title: How World Cup final will be like nothing we have seen before with 30-MINUTE celeb-packed halftime show
items.summary: IT is certainly not the World Cup final we were hoping for – and it will not be like any we have seen before. Fans have already endured Fifa and the Yanks turning matches into a version of American football, with four quarters, kick-off countdowns and shots of celebs in the crowds every few minutes.
